description: In this tutorial, the concept of quasi permissionless consensus is introduced, building on the previously defined dynamic available setting. The speaker explains that in the quasi permissionless setting, all honest players with non-zero stake are assumed to be active, which is crucial for implementing classical BFT protocols. The discussion also covers the importance of on-chain resources and how they relate to consensus decisions, including the selection of committees and transaction sequences. Various proof of work protocols are mentioned as examples of different on-chain resources.
topic_summary: The video elaborates on the quasi permissionless consensus setting, emphasizing the active participation of all honest players with non-zero stake, which is essential for classical BFT protocols. It also explores the role of on-chain resources in consensus mechanisms, including committee selection and transaction processing [t=0:00-3:04].
